title: Sex
questions: What images spring to mind when you hear the word 'sex'?
Do people in your country find it easy or difficult to talk about sex in your country?
How do you think schools should teach sex education?
Is sex before marriage good or bad?
Does your government provide enough information about safe sex?
Why do so many politicians end up in sex scandals?
Why do men seem to want to have sex more than women?
What do you think should be the legal age of consent (when people can legally start having sex)?
Bertrand Russell said: "Civilized people cannot fully satisfy their sexual instinct without love." What do you think of this?
John Updike said: "Sex is like money; only too much is enough." What do you think about this quote?
Do people talk about sex too much?
Who told you about the birds and the bees?
Who's the greatest sex symbol that ever lived?
How bad is extramarital sex?
Do you think sex scenes in movies should be banned on TV?
How wrong is it to pay for sex?
If sex was a colour, what would it be?
What do you think of the attitude towards sex in different countries?
Andy Warhol said: "Sex is more exciting on the screen and between the pages than between the sheets." What do you think about this quote?
Marshall Brain said: "The purpose of sex is to create a baby." What do you think?
Do you think sex before marriage is ok?
How important is sex in a relationship? Why?
What are your views of homosexuality?
Do you think men care more about sex than women?
